name: PySCF (Python Simulations of Chemistry Framework)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Qiming Sun et al.

performance.notes: C/C++ backend; multi-threaded OpenMP; GPU4PySCF for GPU; molecule size limits ab initio

agent_readiness.notes: Ab initio quantum chemistry in Python. from pyscf import gto, scf, cc, mp. mol = gto.M(atom='H 0 0 0; H 0 0 0.74', basis='cc-pVDZ'). mf = scf.RHF(mol).run(). e_hf = mf.e_tot. mp2 = mp.MP2(mf).run(). ccsd = cc.CCSD(mf).run(). e_ccsd_t = ccsd.ccsd_t(). DFT: mf = dft.RKS(mol); mf.xc = 'B3LYP'. MCSCF: casscf. TDDFT. Geometry optimization. Excited states. GTO basis sets. GPU4PySCF extension. Used in ML force fields training data generation.

gotchas: Requires computational chemistry background (HF, MP2, CCSD, DFT theory)
Large molecules (>100 atoms) require significant compute resources
No type annotations — poor IDE support
Basis set selection significantly affects accuracy and cost
best_when: Ab initio quantum chemistry (HF, MP2, CCSD(T), MCSCF)
DFT calculations (B3LYP, PBE0, etc.) for molecular properties
Generating training data for machine learning force fields
When RDKit or ASE alone aren't sufficient for electronic structure
avoid_when: Classical molecular mechanics (use OpenMM or GROMACS)
summary: PySCF is the leading Python quantum chemistry package. gto.M(atom='H...', basis='cc-pVDZ'). scf.RHF().run(). dft.RKS(xc='B3LYP'). mp.MP2(). cc.CCSD(T). MCSCF. TDDFT. Geometry optimization. GPU4PySCF for GPU acceleration. Apache-2.0. Standard for ML force field training data. Requires quantum chemistry background. GPU4PySCF enables GPU acceleration.
